summary: Security audit completed successfully. All 10 backend security tests passed (100%). Verified: security headers on all responses, rate limiting (5/min on login), authentication with founder account, session cookie settings (SameSite=lax, HttpOnly), and protected endpoints require auth.
passed_tests: Security headers on health endpoint (X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
Security headers on protected endpoints (401 responses)
Session cookie has SameSite=lax and HttpOnly flags
Rate limiting triggers 429 after 5 requests per minute
critical_code_review_comments: SecurityHeadersMiddleware correctly adds all 5 required security headers to every response
Rate limiting properly configured with slowapi - 5 requests per minute on /api/auth/local-login
Session cookies use SameSite=lax (prevents CSRF) and HttpOnly (prevents XSS cookie theft)
validated_file_upload function with magic bytes validation is used in 4 file upload endpoints
FILE_MAGIC_BYTES dictionary covers common file types (PDF, images, Office documents)
Password verification uses bcrypt with automatic migration from legacy SHA-256 hashes
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security audit complete. All 4 phases of security fixes verified: Phase 1 (bcrypt passwords), Phase 2 (rate limiting 5/min, SameSite=lax cookies), Phase 3 (magic bytes file validation), Phase 4 (security headers middleware). Rate limiting test exhausts the 5/min limit - wait 60 seconds before running login tests again.

security_headers.X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block - XSS protection for legacy browsers
security_headers.Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=() - restricts browser features

session_cookies.SameSite: lax - prevents CSRF while allowing normal navigation
session_cookies.HttpOnly: true - prevents JavaScript access to cookie

file_upload_validation.supported_types: PDF, JPG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, MP4, MOV, AVI, WEBM, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX

password_security.legacy_migration: Auto-migrates SHA-256 hashes to bcrypt on login
